item.title: With uncertainty around H-1B visa fee, U.S. businesses unsure how to move forward
item.description: Earlier this week, a federal judge invalidated the White House's $100,000 H-1B fee policy in response to a lawsuit brought by 20 states.

item.title: Fans anticipating critical Game 5 of NBA Finals in San Antonio
item.description: Game 5 of the NBA Finals is in San Antonio on Saturday, and the big question is whether the Knicks clinch or the Spurs survive. Game 4 was the most-watched game ever on social media with 3 billion views and counting. Doug Williams reports from San Antonio.

item.title: Business owner says his company relies on skilled foreign worker visas
item.description: This week, a federal judge struck down the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas. The much higher fee was intended to prevent foreign workers from taking American jobs. But some business owners say the U.S. depends on that talent. Shanelle Kaul reports.

item.title: Royal family watches Royal Air Force flyover for king's official birthday
item.description: The Royal family gathered on the balcony at Buckingham Palace on Saturday to greet the crowds and watch a Royal Air Force flyover in celebration of the king. Holly Williams has more from London.

item.title: U.S.-Iran peace deal to be signed Sunday, Trump says
item.description: President Trump said the Strait of Hormuz will be reopened "immediately," once the memorandum of understanding is signed.

item.title: U.S. cruises past Paraguay 4-1 in World Cup opener
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team made easy work of Paraguay in its World Cup opener Friday, defeating the South American side 4-1 at SoFi Stadium.

item.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center, court filing says
item.description: A federal appellate court denied a last-minute attempt by the Trump administration to stop the removal of President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center on Friday.

item.title: Electric performance by U.S. men in first World Cup match
item.description: The heat is on for the World Cup and so is the temperature. Saturday's matches will be played with the mercury in the 80s. Nicole Valdes reports on the U.S.'s dominating debut.

item.title: U.S., Iran say a deal to end the fighting is close
item.description: Despite President Trump's optimism that a deal with Iran could come as early as Sunday, Iranian leaders insist any deal with the White House is still days away. Imtiaz Tyab reports.

item.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center, national park exhibit removals paused
item.description: A crew stripped President Trump's name from the front of the Kennedy Center overnight, after a federal judge rejected the administration's efforts to keep the signage in place. The removal followed a separate ruling that paused a presidential directive to remove signs and exhibits at national parks that cast the U.S. in a "negative light." Olivia Gazis has more.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 14)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Ford recalls over 250K Focus models over engine stall risk
item.description: Ford is recalling more than 250,000 vehicles that were incorrectly repaired under a previous recall meant to fix a problem that caused the engine to stall while driving, according to the National Highway Traffic and Safety Administration.

item.title: A key spy tool expired due to inaction in Congress. Here's what that means.
item.description: Lawmakers have long sounded the alarm about the risks of letting Section 702 expire. But there's debate over what a lapse in the law actually means.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Man bikes from Pittsburgh to Dallas to watch his home team's World Cup match
item.description: A 22-year-old business student from Japan is biking from Pittsburgh to Dallas to see his home team play in the World Cup. KXII-TV reporter Austin Hedgcoth has the story.

item.title: Ukrainian drone attack kills 1, injures 3 in southern Russia
item.description: Ukraine's General Staff said that its forces had hit an oil preparation and pumping station overnight in Russia's Volgograd region.

item.title: NBA star James Harden arrested in Houston for unlawful carrying of a weapon
item.description: NBA star James Harden was booked into jail and released on bond less than 2 hours later.

item.title: Police dress up as World Cup mascots to carry out drug sting
item.description: The officers in mascot costumes used a metal sledgehammer to break down a door to enter with colleagues.

item.title: Armed men kidnap respected security expert in gang-wracked Haiti
item.description: James Boyard is the cabinet director of Haiti's Defense Ministry and also serves as inspector general of Haiti's police.

item.title: At least 1 killed, 9 hospitalized in Texas mass shooting; suspect dead
item.description: The suspect, Victor Mata Villarreal, was wanted for attempted murder of a police officer after shooting at law enforcement during a vehicle chase earlier this week, officials said.

item.title: Trump's changes to history at national parks must be undone, judge rules
item.description: President Trump issued an executive order in March 2025 ordering national parks to not display elements that "inappropriately disparage Americans past or living."

item.title: Trump says U.S. killed Tren de Aragua leader in airstrike in Venezuela
item.description: The U.S. military has killed Niño Guerrero, the alleged leader of Venezuela-based gang Tren de Aragua, President Trump announced Friday.

item.title: DOJ paves way for Paramount Skydance to buy Warner Bros. Discovery
item.description: Paramount Skydance's $110 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery would not harm U.S. consumers or weaken competition, DOJ antitrust enforcers said.

item.title: Elon Musk becomes the world's first trillionaire with SpaceX's IPO
item.description: The SpaceX CEO's fortune on paper now rivals the annual economic output of many countries, according to World Bank data.

item.title: Pentagon releases 3rd batch of UFO files: "Are you seeing this?"
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday released a new group of documents and videos related to UFOs, or UAPs, with 72 more documents, images and recordings.

item.title: FDA authorizes emergency use of drug to fight screwworms in pets
item.description: Nitenpyram is the first generic animal drug authorized to treat screwworms in dogs and cats, according to federal regulators.

item.title: Trump says "settlement" reached on Iran, signing could happen this weekend
item.description: President Trump said earlier Thursday he called off new military strikes on Iran, hours after threatening to escalate the war.

item.title: Markings appear to show "86 47" on National Mall
item.description: A photographer from the Reuters news outlet saw the apparent "86 47" markings from atop the Washington Monument.

item.title: Human remains found in sleeping bag in national park identified 26 years later
item.description: Forensic tests helped identify a man whose remains were found inside a sleeping bag in Washington state in 2000.

item.title: How an Iowa woman became a key witness in her own murder
item.description: The last words spoken by Angela Prichard, 55, an Iowa wife and mother who called 911 to report she was in danger, was the first clue investigators had to identify her killer.
